body.title: D-slow-query-triage-2026-04-22
body.text:
# D-slow-query-triage-2026-04-22

**Summary:** Igor's DB is drowning in slow queries (55k entries in db_queries.log, 20s preparse on trivial input), blocking the "Igor helps with his own code" goal. Fix the top offenders so Igor can respond conversationally.

**Context:** Discovered during live debug session — boot took 3+ minutes, console input unresponsive, 20s preparse on "hello?". The write-side (db_proxy → db_queries.log) and read-side (analyze_slow_queries tool) infrastructure both exist but findings were never surfaced or acted on. Top offenders: 38k scope-backfill UPDATE loop, 9k _migrations re-checks per session, 891 get_attractors full-table-scans, 5.8s wg_meta upsert worst-case.

**Spawned tickets:**
- T-scope-backfill-migration-loop (M, priority 0.85)
- T-migrations-lookup-cache (S, priority 0.80)
- T-get-attractors-tree-walk (M, priority 0.75)
- T-wg-meta-upsert-latency (M, priority 0.65)
- T-slow-query-boot-surface (S, priority 0.55)

**Date:** 2026-04-22
**Status:** open

## Rollup

**Closed at:** 2026-04-22T19:44:55.128412+00:00
**Ticket count:** 5 (all closed)

### Shipped via
- T-scope-backfill-migration-loop (M) — Fix scope-backfill UPDATE firing every connection (38k hits in db_queries.log)  `done` — Superseded by D-sqlite-removal-2026-04-22. The 38k UPDATE hits were historical — Postgres gate (scope_backfill_123 in _migrations) already shipped 2026-04-17. Residual 3 hits/9 days came from the SQLi
- T-migrations-lookup-cache (S) — Cache _migrations table lookups per-process (9,264 hits, avg 121ms)  `done` — 75254143 — module-level _MIGRATION_CACHE in cortex.py + 3 callsite refactors (scope_backfill gate, meaning_to_me gate, _run_schema_migrations). 6 tests pass. Eliminates SELECT 1 FROM _migrations per C
- T-get-attractors-tree-walk (M) — Replace get_attractors() full-table-scan with hot_attractors/TWM read  `done` — d8d80b2b — module-level _ATTRACTOR_CACHE with 60s TTL. Follow-on for proper hot_attractors subsystem flagged in docstring. 5 tests pass.
- T-wg-meta-upsert-latency (M) — Investigate wg_meta upsert latency (5,855ms worst, 143 hits)  `done` — 37665892 — batched word_count upserts (G-WG3 doc_count pattern). _inc_word_count + flush_word_count. Root cause: every index() hit the hot wg_meta row; 500% bloat on 2-row table confirmed pre-fix. 7 t
- T-slow-query-boot-surface (S) — Surface slow-query report proactively on boot (PROC_SLOW_QUERY_REPORT habit)  `done` — 00a0599b — boot_surface_slow_queries + main.py wire (post BOOT_INVENTORY). Top-5 by worst-elapsed pushed to ring as db_diagnostic. Silent on failure. 5 tests.

_Generated by cc_queue.py _decision_rollup. File-stub until T-decisions-into-palace-subtree moves rollups into the memory palace._

## Provenance
Absorbed from the TheIgors.archive lineage 2026-06-28 (D-all-generations-consolidation-2026-06-28). Historical record; original status: unknown.
